

```{r}
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
```

Full merge join .vcf output files loop
.vcf output files are produced using script from Grünewald et al., 2019 Nature
See scripts at https://github.com/aryeelab/RNAseq_BE_editing


```{r}
# Assign Raw and Output directory
vcf_dir <- "PathToRawUnmergedFiles"
output_dir <- "PathToOutputDirectory"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = TRUE)  # create output dir if it doesn't exist

# Provide your pairs as base names (without the .vcf.sorted.vcf_with_coverage.txt suffix)
# Control as first value, Treated as second value
# For example:
# pairs <- list(
#   c("lane8985_AACGTCTG_GCGTCATT_Sample_1_L001", "lane8985_TTGCAGAC_ACCACGAT_Sample_4_L001"),
#   c("lane8985_ACGTTCAG_GCACAACT_Sample_2_L001", "lane8985_CAATGTGG_GTCGAAGA_Sample_5_L001")
# )

pairs <- list(
  c("lane8985_AACGTCTG_GCGTCATT_Sample_1_L001", "lane8985_TTGCAGAC_ACCACGAT_Sample_4_L001"),
  c("lane8985_ACGTTCAG_GCACAACT_Sample_2_L001", "lane8985_CAATGTGG_GTCGAAGA_Sample_5_L001"),
  c("lane8985_CAGTCCAA_GATTACCG_Sample_3_L001", "lane8985_ACTCCATC_CCTTGATC_Sample_6_L001")
)

```


```{r}
# Loop over pairs

for (pair in pairs) {
  control_file <- file.path(vcf_dir, paste0(pair[1], ".vcf.sorted.vcf_with_coverage.txt"))
  treated_file <- file.path(vcf_dir, paste0(pair[2], ".vcf.sorted.vcf_with_coverage.txt"))
  
  # Read files
  treated <- read_tsv(treated_file,
                      col_names = c("poskey","chr","POS","other","REF","ALT","QUAL",
                                    "ref.other","total_reads","A","C","G","T","N"))
  
  control <- read_tsv(control_file,
                      col_names = c("poskey","chr","POS","other","REF","ALT","QUAL",
                                    "ref.other","total_reads","A","C","G","T","N"))
  
  # Merge
  merged <- full_join(treated, control, by = "poskey", suffix = c(".treated", ".control")) %>%
    mutate(
      chr = coalesce(chr.treated, chr.control),
      POS = coalesce(POS.treated, POS.control),
      other = coalesce(other.treated, other.control),
      REF = coalesce(REF.treated, REF.control),
      ALT = coalesce(ALT.treated, ALT.control),
#      QUAL = coalesce(QUAL.treated, QUAL.control),
      ref.other = coalesce(ref.other.treated, ref.other.control)
    )
  
  # Fill numeric NAs with 0
  num_cols <- c("total_reads.treated","A.treated","C.treated","G.treated","T.treated","N.treated",
              "total_reads.control","A.control","C.control","G.control","T.control","N.control",
              "QUAL.treated", "QUAL.control")
  
  for (col in num_cols) {
    if (col %in% colnames(merged)) {
      merged[[col]][is.na(merged[[col]])] <- 0
    }
  }
  
  # Select final column order
  merged <- merged %>%
    select(poskey, chr, POS, other, REF, ALT, QUAL.control, QUAL.treated, ref.other,
           total_reads.treated, A.treated, C.treated, G.treated, T.treated, N.treated,
           total_reads.control, A.control, C.control, G.control, T.control, N.control)
  
  merged <- merged %>%
  mutate(
    read_status = case_when(
      total_reads.treated > 0 & total_reads.control == 0 ~ "Treated_only",
      total_reads.treated == 0 & total_reads.control > 0 ~ "Control_only",
      total_reads.treated > 0 & total_reads.control > 0 ~ "Both",
      total_reads.treated == 0 & total_reads.control == 0 ~ "None"
    )
  )

  
  # Write output
  base_name <- paste0("full_merged_", pair[1])
  output_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0(base_name, ".tsv"))
  write_tsv(merged, output_file)
  
  cat("Merged pair:", pair[1], "+", pair[2], "->", output_file, "\n")
}
```
